_comment: Transverse-field Ising on an open 3-qubit chain: H = -(Z0Z1 + Z1Z2) - 0.8*(X0+X1+X2). The ground state is entangled and is NOT reachable exactly by a shallow hardware-efficient ansatz, so there is real optimization headroom: ground_state_energy is from exact diagonalization; a run gets ranked by how close to it the design lands (lower gap wins). The model designs an ansatz + bound parameters to minimize <H>; the judge re-derives it.
